page: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the DCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, with cache sizes of 48 KB for L1d, 1 MB for L2, and 48 MB for L3. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results position the DC16s_v3 in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with select strong performance highlights. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance scored 1898.0, while multi-core performance reached 30343.46. Memory bandwidth tests showed a read-only small block speed of 1687.72 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 60.51 GB/sec. In Geekbench evaluations, the instance demonstrated strong image processing capabilities with a score of 13806.0, alongside average scores in software compilation and ray tracing. For database operations, Passmark recorded a score of 14827.63, and Redis SET operations achieved 31.87 million operations per second.

The instance exhibits varying performance for language model inference. For a small 135M parameter model, it delivers a strong text generation speed of 316.58 tokens per second and an average prompt processing speed of 2293.61 tokens per second. However, performance drops significantly on larger models, with prompt processing for a 70B model falling into the weak tier at 6.61 tokens per second. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, lack of local storage, and solid memory bandwidth, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, image processing, and moderate-scale web serving tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it provides 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory density. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but supports premium external storage. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for most operations, with notable strengths in image processing and text generation for small language models. It achieves a Redis throughput of 31.87 million operations per second and a Passmark database score of 14827.63. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive applications, database workloads, and moderate computational tasks requiring high RAM-to-vCPU ratios without local storage dependencies.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB of RAM, and premium storage support, delivering balanced performance for database and memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB of RAM, and premium storage support for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high memory density for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Premium storage capable configuration with no local storage included
Strong performance in image processing and small-model text generation
Suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, and web serving
